title: Dependency Graphs, Incremental Builds, Caches, and Watch Mode · Runtime Lens: Watch Coalescing
summary: Changing a layout rebuilds one page, deleting content leaves output behind, cached nodes ignore parser versions, watch events duplicate builds, and a fast incremental result differs from a clean build. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the watch coalescing increment in clean-equivalent incremental dependency engine. Learners progress through pred…
objectives: Treat watch events as hints, debounce bursts, rescan authoritative state, serialize publication, and recover from overflow or missed events.
Propagate changed, added, deleted, renamed, and dependency updates to every affected output and derived collection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-incremental-dependencies-2
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-incremental-dependencies-2.
# Case 7417: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def worked_static_site_incremental_dependencies_2(outputs: int = 20, dependency_nodes: int = 37, invalidated: int = 6, stale_outputs: int = 0, clean_digest_equal: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "readerworked2",
        "source7417",
        "parse2dekrow",
        "routeworked27417",
        "artifact7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if outputs < 1 or dependency_nodes < outputs:
        return False, dependency_nodes, "dependency-shape"
    if invalidated < 1 or invalidated > outputs:
        return False, invalidated, "invalidation-scope"
    if stale_outputs > 0:
        return False, stale_outputs, "stale-output"
    if not clean_digest_equal:
        return False, invalidated, "clean-drift"
    assert dependency_nodes >= outputs
    return True, invalidated, "clean-equivalent-incremental"

# Evidence: ssg-watch-coalescing-1geiuu
# Competency: ssg-watch-coalescing.
# Case 8393: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.

def evidence_ssg_watch_coalescing_1geiuu(outputs: int = 20, dependency_nodes: int = 37, invalidated: int = 6, stale_outputs: int = 0, clean_digest_equal: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1geiuu = "1geiuu-uuieg1-8393"
    assert evidence_variant_1geiuu
    evidence_axes_1geiuu = (
        "reader1geiuu",
        "source8393",
        "parseuuieg1",
        "route1geiuu8393",
        "artifact83931geiuu",
        "recoveryuuieg18393",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1geiuu)) == 6
    if outputs < 1 or dependency_nodes < outputs:
        return False, dependency_nodes, "dependency-shape"
    if invalidated < 1 or invalidated > outputs:
        return False, invalidated, "invalidation-scope"
    if stale_outputs > 0:
        return False, stale_outputs, "stale-output"
    if not clean_digest_equal:
        return False, invalidated, "clean-drift"
    assert dependency_nodes >= outputs
    return True, invalidated, "clean-equivalent-incremental"

options.text: Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the watch coalescing defect evidence boundary.

For repair the watch coalescing defect, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof.
Reconstruct the conditions for Watch Coalescing, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Watch Coalescing model · Theory Watch Coalescing
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Watch Coalescing: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 1856 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to prove dependency-aware incremental output is equivalent to a clean build; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Treat watch events as hints, debounce bursts, rescan authoritative state, serialize publication, and recover from overflow or missed events.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Watch Coalescing model · Theory Watch Coalescing.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Watch Coalescing model · Theory Watch Coalescing, keep Watch Coalescing connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Treat watch events as hints, debounce bursts, rescan authoritative state, serialize publication, and recover from overflow or missed events.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Watch Coalescing model · Theory Watch Coalescing, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Watch Coalescing precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Watch Coalescing model · Theory Watch Coalescing, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 1856 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to prove dependency-aware incremental output is equivalent to a clean build; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Treat watch events as hints, debounce bursts, rescan authoritative state, serialize publication, and recover from overflow or missed events.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Watch Coalescing model · Theory Watch Coalescing, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Every filesystem notification maps one-to-one to a user edit.
